title: leymus coreanus
intro: Leymus coreanus, also known as Korean wild ryegrass, is a type of perennial grass that grows in the mountainous regions of Korea and parts of China. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Leymus coreanus include treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, as well as relieving symptoms of fever and cough. Herbal preparations of Leymus coreanus typically involve infusing the dried grass in hot water or making it into a tea to be consumed for its medicinal properties.
